name: tiger-skills
description: Code-quality (inner loop) and harness-engineering (outer loop) skills that work as one system. code-quality enforces 13 design principles + Python/Rust rules. harness-engineering manages the full agent workflow from session discipline through verification. Each skill cross-references the other at defined handoff points.
author: arkadaz

skills.name: code-quality
skills.description: Inner loop — enforces 13 design principles (SRP, OCP, LSP, DRY), language-specific rules for Python and Rust, independent review agent, typed boundaries (no bare dict/list/set/tuple), structured logging, enums for all fixed choices including factory/registry keys, empty __init__.py policy, no leading-underscore naming, and no-water code. Cross-references harness-engineering for verification pipeline.

skills.name: harness-engineering
skills.description: Outer loop conductor — bootstrap gate, 7-phase protocol (clarify→explore→spec→plan→implement→verify→track), Iron Law verification (never claim done without fresh evidence), rationalization prevention, bite-sized tasks (no placeholders/stubs), subagent-driven development with status protocol (DONE/BLOCKED/NEEDS_CONTEXT), model selection, two-stage review (spec compliance + code quality), TDD (Red-Green-Refactor with testing anti-patterns), systematic debugging (4-phase root cause + defense-in-depth), spec/plan self-review checklists, continuous execution, implementer escalation protocol, session discipline, WIP=1, diagnostic loop, parallel agent dispatch. Cross-references code-quality at implementation steps.
